title: TUI roadmap: annotate after edges on epic lines
description:
internal/tui/roadmap.go renderRoadmapEpicLine (~line 98) renders: <glyph> <id> [closed/total] <title> [gate:<type>] (needs planning) [← blocked by: id1 id2] using roadmapBlockedStyle. RoadmapEpic (internal/query/roadmap.go) now carries After []string — epic-only soft-ordering targets (preference, never a constraint).

Work: append an after annotation "← after: id1 id2" following the blocked-by annotation, in a more muted style than roadmapBlockedStyle (soft edge = weaker visual weight; define e.g. roadmapAfterStyle next to the existing styles, using a dim/overlay color consistent with internal/tui/styles.go + internal/styles conventions). Omit when After is empty. Update the format doc comment above the function.

Test cases (extend the existing TUI roadmap render tests):
- Epic with After ["x1","x2"], no BlockedBy -> line contains "after: x1 x2", no "blocked by".
- Epic with both -> blocked-by annotation precedes after annotation.
- Epic with neither -> unchanged line (no regression on existing 11 render tests).

Files likely touched: internal/tui/roadmap.go, internal/tui/roadmap_test.go.
Run: gofmt -l internal; go build ./...; go test -short -count=1 ./internal/tui/...
acceptance_criteria: TUI roadmap lines render a muted after annotation distinct from blocked-by; render tests cover with/without/mixed; go test ./internal/tui/... passes
closed_reason: Completed: muted after annotation (Overlay0) on TUI roadmap lines, doc comments updated, 2 new tests incl. style-distinctness
